steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries (Standard English Conventions) question. The sentence begins with the interrogative word "Where," so the blank must complete a direct question. A direct question in Standard English requires subject-auxiliary inversion (auxiliary before subject) and a question mark at the end.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘did the word "chortle" come from.’: The word order is correct (auxiliary "did" before subject "the word"), but a period cannot end an interrogative sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the word "chortle" did come from?’: Ends with a question mark, but the subject "the word" comes before the auxiliary "did," which violates the inversion rule for direct questions. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘did the word "chortle" come from?’: Auxiliary "did" precedes the subject "the word" (correct inversion), and the sentence ends with a question mark. This forms a grammatically correct direct question. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘the word "chortle" come from.’: No auxiliary verb at all, no inversion, and ends with a period — fails on every count. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
